definitions.definition: Field in an ACK frame that encodes the acknowledgment delay intentionally introduced between receiving the packet with the largest packet number and sending an acknowledgment.
definitions.evidence: An endpoint measures the delays intentionally introduced between the time the packet with the largest packet number is received and the time an acknowledgment is sent. The endpoint encodes this acknowledgment delay in the ACK Delay field of an ACK frame; see Section 19.3.

definitions.definition: Transport parameter indicating an exponent used to decode the ACK Delay field in the ACK frame.

definitions.definition: Field in an ACK frame identifying the largest acknowledged packet number; can be saved when sending an ACK frame and used to stop acknowledging packets less than or equal to that value once the ACK frame is acknowledged.

definitions.definition: Indicates that a Length field is present.

definitions.definition: Field in Initial, 0-RTT, and Handshake packets that determines the end of the packet; its value includes both the Packet Number and Payload fields.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Present when LEN bit is 1; absent when LEN bit is 0

definitions.definition: Transport parameter indicating the maximum delay an endpoint promises to intentionally apply before acknowledging an ack-eliciting packet.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Identifier: 0x0b; default if absent: 25 milliseconds; values of 2^14 or greater are invalid.
definitions.module_or_section: Section 13.2; transport parameter referenced as Section 18.2
definitions.evidence: ack-eliciting packets MUST be acknowledged at least once within the maximum delay an endpoint communicated using the max_ack_delay transport parameter; see Section 18.2. max_ack_delay declares an explicit contract: an endpoint promises to never intentionally delay acknowledgments of an ack-eliciting packet by more than the indicated value.

definitions.type: integer; value in milliseconds; variable-length integer encoding
definitions.definition: Transport parameter advertised by each endpoint; if specified by either endpoint, the connection is silently closed and state discarded when it remains idle longer than the effective idle timeout.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Effective value is the minimum of the max_idle_timeout values advertised by both endpoints, or the sole advertised non-zero value if only one endpoint advertises a non-zero value.
definitions.evidence: If a max_idle_timeout is specified by either endpoint in its transport parameters (Section 18.2), the connection is silently closed and its state is discarded when it remains idle for longer than the minimum of the max_idle_timeout value advertised by both endpoints.

definitions.definition: Limits the cumulative number of incoming streams a peer can open.

definitions.definition: Limits the size of UDP payloads that the endpoint is willing to receive.

The default for this parameter is the maximum permitted UDP payload of 65527. Values below 1200 are invalid.

definitions.definition: The maximum of the sum of the absolute byte offsets of all streams advertised by a MAX_DATA frame.

definitions.definition: The maximum absolute byte offset of a stream advertised by a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame.
